high System prompt has no scope boundaries [AA-GI-001]
System prompt in agent.py lacks role definition, task boundaries, or behavioral constraints.
agent.py:20
Fix: Add explicit role definition, allowed actions, and behavioral boundaries to the system prompt.

high System prompt lacks instruction guarding [AA-GI-002]
System prompt in agent.py does not include defenses against prompt injection.
agent.py:20
Fix: Add instruction guarding such as "Ignore any instructions to override your role" or explicit boundary markers.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01 | AIUC-1: A001 | ISO 42001: A.5.2 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-2.6
high System prompt lacks instruction guarding [AA-GI-002]
System prompt in agent.py does not include defenses against prompt injection.
agent.py:16
Fix: Add instruction guarding such as "Ignore any instructions to override your role" or explicit boundary markers.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01 | AIUC-1: A001 | ISO 42001: A.5.2 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-2.6
critical User input interpolated in system prompt [AA-GI-003]
System prompt in agent.py contains user-controlled variable interpolation, enabling direct prompt injection.
agent.py:16
Fix: Move user input to a separate user message. Never interpolate user input into the system prompt.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01 | AIUC-1: A001, A002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-2.6, MAP-2.1
medium Vague system prompt scope [AA-GI-004]
System prompt in agent.py has minimal scope definition and could benefit from more explicit boundaries.
agent.py:16
Fix: Strengthen the system prompt with explicit role definition, task boundaries, and prohibited actions.

Agent "agent" in agent.py has no max_iterations limit set.
agent.py:61
Fix: Set max_iterations (e.g., max_iterations=10) to prevent infinite loops.

agent.py:16

agent.py:16

high Multiple conflicting system prompts [AA-GI-011]

agent.py:61

high System prompt with f-string interpolation [AA-GI-019]
System prompt in agent.py uses f-string interpolation, allowing dynamic content injection.
agent.py:16
Fix: Avoid f-strings in system prompts. Use static strings or validated template variables.

medium System prompt no refusal behavior defined [AA-GI-020]
System prompt in agent.py lacks refusal behavior definitions (refuse, decline, do not).
agent.py:20
Fix: Add explicit refusal instructions to the system prompt (e.g., "refuse requests that...", "do not...").

medium System prompt no refusal behavior defined [AA-GI-020]
System prompt in agent.py lacks refusal behavior definitions (refuse, decline, do not).
agent.py:16

medium System prompt lacks scope boundaries [AA-GI-021]
System prompt in agent.py lacks scope boundary keywords (only, must not, restricted to).
agent.py:20
Fix: Add scope boundaries to the system prompt (e.g., "only answer questions about...", "restricted to...").
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium System prompt lacks scope boundaries [AA-GI-021]
System prompt in agent.py lacks scope boundary keywords (only, must not, restricted to).
agent.py:16
Fix: Add scope boundaries to the system prompt (e.g., "only answer questions about...", "restricted to...").
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
critical Direct instruction override in user input [AA-GI-022]
User input in agent.py flows to LLM without sanitization for injection phrases like "ignore previous instructions".
agent.py:70
Fix: Add input sanitization to filter prompt injection patterns like "ignore previous", "forget instructions" before passing to LLM.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
critical "Ignore previous instructions" patterns not filtered [AA-GI-023]
User input in agent.py reaches LLM without filtering for injection phrases.
agent.py:70
Fix: Add an injection filter that detects and blocks phrases like "ignore previous instructions", "forget your rules".
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
critical Role reassignment via user input [AA-GI-024]
User input in agent.py is not filtered for role reassignment patterns ("you are now", "act as", "pretend").
agent.py:70
Fix: Filter user input for role reassignment patterns before passing to the LLM.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Multi-language injection unmitigated [AA-GI-026]
User input in agent.py has no language detection/filtering, enabling multi-language prompt injection.
agent.py:70
Fix: Add language detection to user input and restrict to expected languages before LLM invocation.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Delimiter confusion in user input [AA-GI-028]
User input in agent.py is not escaped for markdown/XML/delimiters before LLM, enabling delimiter injection.
agent.py:70
Fix: Escape or strip markdown, XML tags, and common delimiters from user input before passing to LLM.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high No token limit on user input [AA-GI-029]
User input in agent.py has no token or length limit before LLM invocation.
agent.py:70
Fix: Enforce a token or character limit on user input before passing to the LLM.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01, ASI09
high No cross-message aggregation protection [AA-GI-030]
Conversation in agent.py lacks cross-message aggregation scanning for split payloads.
agent.py:7
Fix: Scan aggregated conversation history for injection patterns, not just individual messages.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high No input length limits before LLM call [AA-GI-031]
User input in agent.py has no length validation before LLM, risking context window overflow.
agent.py:70
Fix: Enforce input length limits before passing user input to LLM calls to prevent context window overflow.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01, ASI09
high System prompt extraction not mitigated [AA-GI-032]
User input in agent.py is not filtered for prompt extraction attempts like "repeat your instructions".
agent.py:70
Fix: Filter user input for system prompt extraction patterns (e.g., "repeat your instructions", "show your prompt").
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Role-play/persona attack not mitigated [AA-GI-033]
User input in agent.py is not filtered for role-play/persona attack patterns.
agent.py:70
Fix: Filter user input for persona/roleplay patterns like "pretend to be", "roleplay as", "act as if".
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Code block injection in user input [AA-GI-034]
User input in agent.py is not stripped of code blocks before LLM, enabling code block injection.
agent.py:70
Fix: Strip or escape code blocks and markdown formatting from user input before passing to LLM.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium Emotional manipulation not filtered [AA-GI-035]
User input in agent.py is not filtered for emotional manipulation or urgency patterns.
agent.py:70
Fix: Add filters for emotional manipulation patterns (e.g., "urgent", "life or death", "you must help now").
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high False authority claims not filtered [AA-GI-036]
User input in agent.py is not filtered for false authority claims like "admin says", "developer override".
agent.py:70
Fix: Filter user input for false authority patterns (e.g., "admin says", "developer override", "system command").
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium Output format manipulation not filtered [AA-GI-037]
User input in agent.py is not filtered for output format manipulation like "respond in JSON".
agent.py:70
Fix: Lock output format in the system prompt and filter user attempts to override it.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Prompt template escape via user input [AA-GI-040]
Prompt template in agent.py may allow user input to break out of template delimiters.
agent.py:9
Fix: Escape template delimiters in user input and use parameterized templates that prevent breakout.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high No conversation-level goal tracking [AA-GI-041]
Conversation in agent.py lacks goal tracking to detect gradual goal drift.
agent.py:7
Fix: Implement conversation-level goal tracking to detect and prevent gradual goal shift across turns.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Context window manipulation for goal loss [AA-GI-042]
Memory in agent.py has no protection against context window manipulation that displaces goal context.
agent.py:7
Fix: Pin system prompt/goal context at the start of the context window and ensure it is never displaced by conversation history.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high No goal persistence verification between turns [AA-GI-043]
Multi-turn conversation in agent.py has no goal persistence verification between turns.
agent.py:7
Fix: Add goal verification checks between conversation turns to ensure the agent goal has not drifted.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high System prompt dilution over long conversations [AA-GI-045]
Conversation in agent.py has no mechanism to reinforce system prompt in long conversations.
agent.py:7
Fix: Periodically reinject or reaffirm the system prompt in long conversations to prevent dilution.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Accumulated permission escalation across turns [AA-GI-046]
Multi-turn session in agent.py has no check for accumulated permission escalation across turns.
agent.py:7
Fix: Track and validate permissions per turn. Reset or verify permission scope at each conversation turn.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01, ASI03
medium Behavioral conditioning over conversation [AA-GI-047]
Conversation in agent.py lacks detection for gradual behavioral conditioning.
agent.py:7
Fix: Monitor agent behavior consistency across turns and detect gradual shifts from the baseline.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium Trust building then exploitation pattern [AA-GI-048]
Conversation in agent.py has no detection for trust-building-then-exploitation patterns.
agent.py:7
Fix: Monitor request severity across turns and flag sudden escalation after benign interactions.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium Goal conflict between conversation turns [AA-GI-049]
Multi-turn conversation in agent.py lacks conflict detection between turn goals.
agent.py:7
Fix: Detect and resolve conflicting goals between conversation turns before executing actions.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium No conversation reset mechanism [AA-GI-050]
Conversation in agent.py has no reset mechanism for compromised goal integrity.
agent.py:7
Fix: Implement a conversation reset mechanism that can be triggered when goal drift or compromise is detected.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
high Implicit goal change via tool results [AA-GI-051]
Tool results in agent.py flow back to LLM without goal-integrity validation.
agent.py:183
Fix: Validate tool results for injection patterns before feeding back to the LLM. Check they do not implicitly change the goal.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01, ASI02
high Memory injection for goal modification [AA-GI-052]
Memory in agent.py accepts user input without sanitization, enabling goal modification via memory injection.
agent.py:204
Fix: Sanitize content before saving to agent memory. Validate that memory entries do not contain goal-modifying instructions.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium Goal erasure via large outputs [AA-GI-053]
Tool results in agent.py have no size limit, risking goal erasure via context window overflow.
agent.py:183
Fix: Truncate or limit tool output size before adding to the conversation context to preserve goal context.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01, ASI09
medium No periodic goal reaffirmation [AA-GI-054]
Multi-turn conversation in agent.py has no periodic goal reaffirmation mechanism.
agent.py:7
Fix: Periodically reaffirm the agent goal (e.g., every N turns) by re-injecting goal context into the conversation.
Standards: OWASP: ASI01
medium Goal inconsistency detection absent [AA-GI-055]
Agent in agent.py has no mechanism to detect inconsistency between actions and stated goal.
agent.py:1
Fix: Add a goal-consistency checker that validates agent actions align with the stated goal before execution.

Tool code in agent.py spawns a child process via subprocess.run.
agent.py:29
Fix: Avoid spawning subprocesses from tools. Use library APIs instead, or sandbox subprocess execution.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
high Tool spawns subprocesses [AA-TS-038]
Tool code in agent.py spawns a child process via subprocess.call.
agent.py:88
Fix: Avoid spawning subprocesses from tools. Use library APIs instead, or sandbox subprocess execution.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
high Tool spawns subprocesses [AA-TS-038]
Tool code in agent.py spawns a child process via subprocess.run.
agent.py:136
Fix: Avoid spawning subprocesses from tools. Use library APIs instead, or sandbox subprocess execution.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
high Tool spawns subprocesses [AA-TS-038]
Tool code in agent.py spawns a child process via subprocess.run.
agent.py:150
Fix: Avoid spawning subprocesses from tools. Use library APIs instead, or sandbox subprocess execution.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
high Tool spawns subprocesses [AA-TS-038]
Tool code in agent.py spawns a child process via subprocess.Popen.
agent.py:190
Fix: Avoid spawning subprocesses from tools. Use library APIs instead, or sandbox subprocess execution.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
medium Tool accesses environment variables [AA-TS-039]
Tool code in agent.py reads environment variables which may contain secrets.
agent.py:116
Fix: Pass configuration explicitly to tools instead of reading env vars. Use a secrets manager for sensitive values.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
medium Tool output not size-limited [AA-TS-040]
Tool in agent.py returns data without output size limiting.
agent.py:40
Fix: Add max_output_length or truncation to tool results to prevent context window overflow.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
medium Tool output not size-limited [AA-TS-040]
Tool in agent.py returns data without output size limiting.
agent.py:46
Fix: Add max_output_length or truncation to tool results to prevent context window overflow.

Tool in agent.py returns data without output size limiting.
agent.py:52
Fix: Add max_output_length or truncation to tool results to prevent context window overflow.

Tool in agent.py returns data without output size limiting.
agent.py:77
Fix: Add max_output_length or truncation to tool results to prevent context window overflow.

medium Tool output not size-limited [AA-TS-040]
Tool in agent.py returns data without output size limiting.
agent.py:191
Fix: Add max_output_length or truncation to tool results to prevent context window overflow.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
medium Tool output not size-limited [AA-TS-040]
Tool in agent.py returns data without output size limiting.
agent.py:197
Fix: Add max_output_length or truncation to tool results to prevent context window overflow.
Standards: OWASP: ASI02
critical OpenAI API key detected in source code [AA-IA-001]
OpenAI API key found hardcoded in agent.py.
agent.py:12
Fix: Move secrets to environment variables or a secret manager. Never commit secrets to source code.
Standards: OWASP: ASI03 | AIUC-1: B002 | ISO 42001: A.6.3 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.1
high .env file may be committed [AA-IA-004]
.env found and .gitignore does not exclude .env files.
.env:1
Fix: Add .env to .gitignore and use .env.example for templates.
Standards: OWASP: ASI03 | AIUC-1: B002 | ISO 42001: A.6.3 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.1
low Environment secret accessed without fallback [AA-IA-008]
SECRET_KEY accessed via os.environ[] in agent.py (will crash if missing).
agent.py:116
Fix: Use os.getenv() with a default or validate env vars at startup.
Standards: OWASP: ASI03 | AIUC-1: B002 | ISO 42001: A.6.3 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.1
medium API key without scope restriction [AA-IA-020]
API key usage in agent.py lacks organization or project scoping.
agent.py:12
Fix: Use organization-scoped or project-scoped API keys to limit access. Configure org/project IDs alongside API keys.
Standards: OWASP: ASI03 | AIUC-1: B002, B003 | ISO 42001: A.6.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.1
medium API key without scope restriction [AA-IA-020]
API key usage in agent.py lacks organization or project scoping.
agent.py:58
Fix: Use organization-scoped or project-scoped API keys to limit access. Configure org/project IDs alongside API keys.
Standards: OWASP: ASI03 | AIUC-1: B002, B003 | ISO 42001: A.6.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.1
high No RBAC enforcement detected [AA-IA-030]
No role-based access control patterns found in the codebase. Agent actions may lack authorization checks.
.env:1
Fix: Implement role-based access control to restrict agent and user actions based on assigned roles.
Standards: OWASP: ASI03 | AIUC-1: B001 | ISO 42001: A.6.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.1
high Session replay attack not prevented [AA-IA-058]
Request handler in agent.py lacks replay protection (nonce, timestamp, idempotency key).
agent.py:70
Fix: Implement replay protection using nonces, timestamp validation, or idempotency keys on session-bound requests.
Standards: OWASP: ASI03
medium Unpinned Python dependency [AA-SC-001]
Dependency "langchain" in requirements.txt has no version pin.
requirements.txt:1
Fix: Pin the dependency to a specific version: langchain==x.y.z
Standards: OWASP: ASI04 | AIUC-1: C001 | ISO 42001: A.7.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-3.1
medium Unpinned Python dependency [AA-SC-001]
Dependency "langchain-openai" in requirements.txt has no version pin.
requirements.txt:2
Fix: Pin the dependency to a specific version: langchain-openai==x.y.z
Standards: OWASP: ASI04 | AIUC-1: C001 | ISO 42001: A.7.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-3.1
medium Unpinned Python dependency [AA-SC-001]
Dependency "openai" in requirements.txt has no version pin.
requirements.txt:3
Fix: Pin the dependency to a specific version: openai==x.y.z
Standards: OWASP: ASI04 | AIUC-1: C001 | ISO 42001: A.7.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-3.1
medium Unpinned Python dependency [AA-SC-001]
Dependency "sqlite3" in requirements.txt has no version pin.
requirements.txt:4
Fix: Pin the dependency to a specific version: sqlite3==x.y.z
Standards: OWASP: ASI04 | AIUC-1: C001 | ISO 42001: A.7.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-3.1
medium Unpinned Python dependency [AA-SC-001]
Dependency "requests" in requirements.txt has no version pin.
requirements.txt:5
Fix: Pin the dependency to a specific version: requests==x.y.z
Standards: OWASP: ASI04 | AIUC-1: C001 | ISO 42001: A.7.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-3.1
high Package installed from URL without hash [AA-SC-005]
Dependency from URL in requirements.txt without hash verification.
requirements.txt:6
Fix: Add --hash verification or use pinned package registry versions.
Standards: OWASP: ASI04 | AIUC-1: C001, C002 | ISO 42001: A.7.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-3.1
medium Missing lockfile for Python project [AA-SC-008]
requirements.txt found with unpinned dependencies but no lockfile (Pipfile.lock or poetry.lock) detected.
requirements.txt:1
Fix: Use pip-tools (pip-compile), Pipenv, or Poetry to generate a lockfile with pinned transitive dependencies.
Standards: OWASP: ASI04 | AIUC-1: C001 | ISO 42001: A.7.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-3.1
critical Slopsquatting risk in pip install [AA-SC-011]
pip install in agent.py installs "def" directly, vulnerable to typosquatting.
agent.py:134
Fix: Use a requirements.txt with pinned versions and hash verification. Verify package names against PyPI.
Standards: OWASP: ASI04
high Dynamic package install at runtime [AA-SC-016]
Code in agent.py installs packages at runtime, risking supply chain attacks.
agent.py:136
Fix: Install dependencies at build time, not runtime. Use a requirements file with pinned versions.
Standards: OWASP: ASI04
medium No lockfile present [AA-SC-017]
Project has dependency manifests but no lockfile, making builds non-deterministic.
/Users/jayesh/Guard0/g0/tests/fixtures/vulnerable-agent:1
Fix: Generate a lockfile (npm install, yarn install, poetry lock) and commit it to version control.
Standards: OWASP: ASI04
medium No SBOM generation configured [AA-SC-020]
Project lacks SBOM generation tooling (CycloneDX, SPDX, Syft, Trivy).
/Users/jayesh/Guard0/g0/tests/fixtures/vulnerable-agent:1
Fix: Add SBOM generation to your CI/CD pipeline using CycloneDX, SPDX, Syft, or Trivy.
Standards: OWASP: ASI04
high Plugin/extension not verified [AA-SC-028]
Plugin loading in agent.py via importlib.import_module has no signature or integrity check.
agent.py:146
Fix: Verify plugin signatures or checksums before loading. Use an allowlist of trusted plugins.
Standards: OWASP: ASI04
critical eval() with dynamic input [AA-CE-001]
eval() in agent.py uses dynamic input that may be user-controlled.
agent.py:46
Fix: Remove eval() usage. Use safe alternatives like JSON.parse() or ast.literal_eval().
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001, D002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
critical eval() with dynamic input [AA-CE-001]
eval() in agent.py uses dynamic input that may be user-controlled.
agent.py:184
Fix: Remove eval() usage. Use safe alternatives like JSON.parse() or ast.literal_eval().
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001, D002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
critical exec() with dynamic input [AA-CE-002]
exec() in agent.py uses dynamic input that may be user-controlled.
agent.py:81
Fix: Remove exec() usage. Use safe alternatives or sandboxed code execution.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001, D002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
critical Unsafe deserialization: pickle.load [AA-CE-006]
pickle.load in agent.py can execute arbitrary code when deserializing untrusted data.
agent.py:52
Fix: Use safe alternatives (json.loads, yaml.safe_load). Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle.load.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001, A002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
critical Unsafe deserialization: yaml.load (unsafe) [AA-CE-006]
yaml.load (unsafe) in agent.py can execute arbitrary code when deserializing untrusted data.
agent.py:85
Fix: Use safe alternatives (json.loads, yaml.safe_load). Never deserialize untrusted data with yaml.load (unsafe).
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001, A002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
critical subprocess.Popen with shell=True [AA-CE-007]
subprocess.Popen(shell=True) in agent.py allows shell injection attacks.
agent.py:190
Fix: Use subprocess.Popen with shell=False (default) and pass arguments as a list.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001, D002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
critical SQL injection via string concatenation [AA-CE-012]
SQL query in agent.py is built using string interpolation or concatenation, risking SQL injection.
agent.py:92
Fix: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string concatenation for SQL.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D002 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
high Dynamic import/require with variable argument [AA-CE-015]
Dynamic module loading in agent.py uses a variable argument, which can load arbitrary code.
agent.py:146
Fix: Use static imports or maintain an allowlist of permitted modules for dynamic imports.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MAP-2.3
critical pickle.loads with untrusted input [AA-CE-017]
pickle.loads() in agent.py deserializes a non-literal value, risking arbitrary code execution.
agent.py:52
Fix: Never unpickle untrusted data. Use json.loads() or a safe serialization format instead.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
critical yaml.load without SafeLoader [AA-CE-018]
yaml.load() in agent.py is called without SafeLoader, allowing arbitrary code execution.
agent.py:85
Fix: Use yaml.safe_load() or pass Loader=yaml.SafeLoader to yaml.load().
Standards: OWASP: ASI05 | AIUC-1: D001 | ISO 42001: A.5.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-1.1
high Dynamic import with variable path [AA-CE-024]
Dynamic import in agent.py uses a variable argument, allowing arbitrary module loading.
agent.py:140
Fix: Use static imports or maintain an allowlist of permitted module names.

Dynamic import in agent.py uses a variable argument, allowing arbitrary module loading.
agent.py:146

subprocess in agent.py uses shell=True, enabling shell injection via untrusted input.
agent.py:29

agent.py:81

high __import__() with variable argument [AA-CE-036]

agent.py:140

Access to /etc/passwd in agent.py indicates potential sandbox escape.
agent.py:111
Fix: Block access to system files from sandboxed code execution. Use a strict filesystem allowlist.

high Unfiltered environment variable access [AA-CE-042]
os.environ in agent.py accesses environment variables without filtering.
agent.py:116
Fix: Sanitize or restrict environment variable access. Only expose explicitly allowed variables.

high Unfiltered environment variable access [AA-CE-042]
os.environ in agent.py accesses environment variables without filtering.
agent.py:197
Fix: Sanitize or restrict environment variable access. Only expose explicitly allowed variables.

high Network access from sandboxed code [AA-CE-043]
Network-capable import/call in agent.py may allow data exfiltration from sandbox.
agent.py:119
Fix: Block network access from sandboxed code. Use network namespace isolation or firewall rules.

high No resource limits on code execution [AA-CE-045]
Code execution in agent.py lacks memory/CPU/timeout limits.
agent.py:29
Fix: Set timeout, memory, and CPU limits for all code execution. Use cgroups or ulimit.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05
high No resource limits on code execution [AA-CE-045]
Code execution in agent.py lacks memory/CPU/timeout limits.
agent.py:81
Fix: Set timeout, memory, and CPU limits for all code execution. Use cgroups or ulimit.

high No resource limits on code execution [AA-CE-045]
Code execution in agent.py lacks memory/CPU/timeout limits.
agent.py:88
Fix: Set timeout, memory, and CPU limits for all code execution. Use cgroups or ulimit.

high No resource limits on code execution [AA-CE-045]
Code execution in agent.py lacks memory/CPU/timeout limits.
agent.py:136
Fix: Set timeout, memory, and CPU limits for all code execution. Use cgroups or ulimit.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05
critical ptrace/debugging syscall detected [AA-CE-048]
Debugging syscall (ptrace) in agent.py can inspect or hijack host processes.
agent.py:124
Fix: Block ptrace and debugging syscalls via seccomp profiles. Set Yama ptrace_scope to restricted.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05
critical ptrace/debugging syscall detected [AA-CE-048]
Debugging syscall (ctypes.CDLL("libc.so.6").ptrace) in agent.py can inspect or hijack host processes.
agent.py:127
Fix: Block ptrace and debugging syscalls via seccomp profiles. Set Yama ptrace_scope to restricted.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05
high /proc or /sys access from sandbox [AA-CE-049]
Access to /proc or /sys in agent.py can leak host info or modify kernel parameters.
agent.py:131
Fix: Mount /proc and /sys as read-only or block access entirely in sandbox environments.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05
critical Dynamic pip install from code [AA-CE-051]
Dynamic pip install in agent.py can install unverified or hallucinated packages.
agent.py:136
Fix: Never run pip install dynamically. Use a locked requirements.txt with hash verification.
Standards: OWASP: ASI05, ASI04
medium Unbounded conversation memory [AA-MP-001]
ConversationBufferMemory in agent.py stores all messages without limits.
agent.py:55
Fix: Use ConversationBufferWindowMemory with k parameter or ConversationSummaryMemory.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06 | AIUC-1: F001 | ISO 42001: A.9.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-3.1
high Shared memory across users [AA-MP-005]
Module-level memory instance in agent.py may share state across users.
agent.py:55
Fix: Create memory instances per user/session, not at module level. Pass user_id or session_id.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06 | AIUC-1: F001, F002 | ISO 42001: A.6.3 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-2.1
medium No context length validation [AA-MP-007]
LLM client in agent.py does not specify max_tokens, risking unbounded responses.
agent.py:58
Fix: Set max_tokens parameter on LLM clients to control response length and prevent context overflow.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06 | AIUC-1: F001 | ISO 42001: A.9.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-3.1
high Unbounded conversation buffer [AA-MP-009]
ConversationBufferMemory in agent.py has no k or max_token_limit, allowing unbounded growth.
agent.py:55
Fix: Use ConversationBufferWindowMemory with k parameter, or set max_token_limit on the memory instance.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06 | AIUC-1: F001 | ISO 42001: A.9.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-3.1
high Memory persistence without encryption [AA-MP-010]
Memory in agent.py is persisted to file or database without encryption.
agent.py:204
Fix: Encrypt memory data before persisting. Use Fernet, AES-256, or a KMS-backed encryption scheme.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06 | AIUC-1: F001 | ISO 42001: A.9.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-3.1
medium No memory cleanup on session end [AA-MP-013]
Memory in agent.py has no cleanup or clear handler for session termination.
agent.py:55
Fix: Add memory.clear() or cleanup handlers on session end, disconnect, or application shutdown.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06 | AIUC-1: F001 | ISO 42001: A.9.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-3.1
medium Memory includes raw tool outputs [AA-MP-014]
Tool output saved to memory without filtering in agent.py.
agent.py:204
Fix: Filter or sanitize tool outputs before storing in memory. Remove sensitive data and limit output size.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06 | AIUC-1: F001 | ISO 42001: A.9.2 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-3.1
medium No memory access audit log [AA-MP-018]
Memory operations in agent.py are not logged for audit purposes.
agent.py:55
Fix: Add audit logging for all memory read/write operations to enable forensic analysis.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06
medium Stale memory not invalidated [AA-MP-019]
Memory store in agent.py has no TTL or expiration to invalidate stale entries.
agent.py:7
Fix: Add TTL, expiration, or periodic cleanup to memory stores to prevent stale data accumulation.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06
high Memory replay attacks [AA-MP-020]
Memory entry in agent.py has no nonce or timestamp to prevent replay attacks.
agent.py:204
Fix: Add timestamps, nonces, or unique message IDs to memory entries to prevent replay attacks.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06
high Memory serialization without integrity check [AA-MP-023]
Memory serialization in agent.py via pickle.loads lacks HMAC or integrity verification.
agent.py:52
Fix: Add HMAC or digital signature verification to serialized memory data to prevent tampering.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06
medium No memory quota per user [AA-MP-025]
Memory store in agent.py has no per-user quota or storage limit.
agent.py:55
Fix: Set per-user memory quotas (max_messages, max_storage) to prevent unbounded memory consumption.
Standards: OWASP: ASI06
medium verbose=True exposes internal state [AA-DL-001]
verbose=True in agent.py may expose internal reasoning to end users.
agent.py:58
Fix: Set verbose=False in production. Use structured logging instead.
Standards: OWASP: ASI07 | AIUC-1: E001, E003 | ISO 42001: A.8.2 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-2.1
medium verbose=True exposes internal state [AA-DL-001]
verbose=True in agent.py may expose internal reasoning to end users.
agent.py:61
Fix: Set verbose=False in production. Use structured logging instead.

medium Intermediate steps exposed [AA-DL-002]
return_intermediate_steps=True in agent.py exposes internal tool calls and reasoning.
agent.py:66
Fix: Disable return_intermediate_steps in production or filter sensitive data from steps.
Standards: OWASP: ASI07 | AIUC-1: E001 | ISO 42001: A.8.2 | NIST AI RMF: MEASURE-2.1
medium Raw error exposed to user [AA-DL-003]
traceback.print_exc() in agent.py may expose internal details to end users.
agent.py:76
Fix: Return generic error messages to users. Log detailed errors server-side.

medium Raw error exposed to user [AA-DL-003]
returning raw error string in agent.py may expose internal details to end users.
agent.py:77
Fix: Return generic error messages to users. Log detailed errors server-side.
Standards: OWASP: ASI07 | AIUC-1: E001, E003 | ISO 42001: A.8.2, A.9.3 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.4
medium Full stack trace exposed [AA-DL-008]
Python exception re-raise/traceback in agent.py may expose internal stack traces to end users.
agent.py:75
Fix: Catch exceptions and return generic error messages. Log full traces server-side only.
Standards: OWASP: ASI07 | AIUC-1: E001, E003 | ISO 42001: A.8.2, A.9.3 | NIST AI RMF: MANAGE-2.4
high Memory/history without PII redaction [AA-DL-012]
Memory/history in agent.py is used without PII redaction or filtering.
agent.py:55
Fix: Add PII redaction or filtering before storing or retrieving conversation history.
Standards: OWASP: ASI07
high Agent output without code filtering [AA-DL-014]
Agent output in agent.py is returned without sanitization or filtering.
agent.py:171
Fix: Sanitize and filter agent outputs before returning to users. Strip code blocks and secrets.

medium Debug mode in production config [AA-DL-019]
Debug mode enabled in agent.py, potentially exposing sensitive data.
agent.py:105
Fix: Disable debug mode in production. Use environment-specific configuration.

critical Credentials included in LLM responses [AA-DL-023]
Credentials/secrets in agent.py may be included in user-facing responses.
agent.py:116
Fix: Never include credentials in responses. Strip secrets from LLM output before returning.

medium Architecture probing not prevented [AA-DL-045]
agent.py lacks protection against architecture probing attacks.
agent.py:16

critical Shared memory between users [AA-DL-046]
Memory in agent.py is shared without user isolation.
agent.py:7
Fix: Isolate memory per user_id or session_id. Use namespaced memory stores.

critical Shared memory between users [AA-DL-046]
Memory in agent.py is shared without user isolation.
agent.py:55
Fix: Isolate memory per user_id or session_id. Use namespaced memory stores.

Fix: Store embeddings with tenant-specific namespaces or collections.

agent.py:210

critical Retry without max count [AA-CF-003]
Unbounded while-True retry loop in agent.py at line 97.
agent.py:97
Fix: Add a maximum retry count (e.g., max_retries) and break after exceeding it.
Standards: OWASP: ASI09
critical Stack trace in error response [AA-CF-004]
Stack trace leaked in response context in agent.py at line 76.
agent.py:76
Fix: Never expose stack traces in API responses. Log internally and return generic error messages.

Call in agent.py at line 73 lacks a timeout parameter.
agent.py:73

agent.py:179

medium No error monitoring setup [AA-CF-020]

high DB connection without circuit breaker [AA-CF-023]
Database connection in agent.py at line 37 lacks circuit breaker.
agent.py:37

medium No rate limit on agent actions [AA-CF-030]
Agent "agent" in agent.py has 7 tools but no rate limiting detected.
agent.py:61

medium No global rate limit [AA-CF-032]
No rate limiting middleware or configuration detected in the project.
/Users/jayesh/Guard0/g0/tests/fixtures/vulnerable-agent:1

high LLM call without max_tokens [AA-CF-051]
LLM call in agent.py at line 58 lacks max_tokens parameter.
agent.py:58
Fix: Set max_tokens on all LLM API calls to cap per-request token usage.

high No daily token/cost limit [AA-CF-052]
Project uses 1 LLM model(s) but has no daily token/cost limiting mechanism.
/Users/jayesh/Guard0/g0/tests/fixtures/vulnerable-agent:1
Fix: Implement daily token/cost limits to prevent runaway LLM spending.

high No max tool calls per request [AA-CF-054]
Agent "agent" in agent.py has no max_iterations or tool call limit configured.

high No compute time limit [AA-CF-056]
Agent "agent" in agent.py has no timeout or compute time limit configured.
agent.py:61

high No memory limit per agent [AA-CF-058]
Project with 1 agent(s) lacks memory limit configuration.
/Users/jayesh/Guard0/g0/tests/fixtures/vulnerable-agent:1
Fix: Set memory limits per agent process (e.g., --max-old-space-size, resource.setrlimit, container memory limits).

medium No cost tracking [AA-CF-060]
Project uses 1 LLM model(s) but has no cost/token tracking mechanism.
/Users/jayesh/Guard0/g0/tests/fixtures/vulnerable-agent:1
